symptoms.symptom_name: Intense generalised pruritus with an itch-scratch cycle

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pruritus is the cardinal feature of atopic dermatitis and drives the excoriation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ill-defined erythematous scaly plaques in a flexural distribution (antecubital and popliteal fossae, neck, wrists)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Flexural, ill-defined scaly plaques on an atopic background are the classic morphology and distribution of atopic dermatitis.
Flexural predominance with finger-web sparing argues against the web-space/truncal pattern of scabies.

symptoms.symptom_name: Excoriation with serous weeping and cracked skin in the creases

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Broken, weeping skin is the portal for secondary Staphylococcus aureus infection — raises the infection risk that the safety-net addresses.

symptoms.symptom_name: Xerosis and chronic lichenification on the wrists

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Baseline dryness and lichenification reflect chronic barrier dysfunction and repeated scratching — hallmarks of longstanding atopic dermatitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Personal and family history of atopy (asthma, allergic rhinitis) with cold-dry-weather and post-viral flare triggers

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The atopic triad and recognised environmental/viral triggers strongly support an endogenous atopic dermatitis flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: No honey-coloured crusting, no spreading cellulitic erythema, no fluctuance, no tender lymphadenopathy, afebrile

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of crusting, spreading erythema, fluctuance, lymphadenopathy and fever makes established bacterial superinfection unlikely at presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: No monomorphic punched-out erosions or clustered vesicles, no systemic fever or malaise

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of the characteristic punched-out monomorphic morphology and systemic upset makes eczema herpeticum unlikely now — but it cannot be excluded remotely, so it stays a safety-net trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound sleep deprivation and acute psychological distress from the itch

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe nocturnal itch causing insomnia reflects flare severity and justifies night-time itch control, but is non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs (BP 138/86, HR 98, Temp 37.1, RR 16, SpO2 99%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal, afebrile vitals confirm the patient is systemically well and argue against systemic infection — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers 'systemically well' from patient-reported absence of fever, not a measured value.
